jobs.title: Uganda Field Operations Regional Lead- Central Region

metadata.name: Expected Manager (you can leave this blank)
Referral Cheat Sheet
metadata.value:
Here is what to consider when thinking about who to refer:
"FP&A (Financial Planning & Analysis): You aren’t just recording what happened; you’re predicting what will happen. This involves variance analysis—explaining why the actual spend didn't match the budget.

Grant Lifecycle Management: This is the ""Grants Finance"" side. You’ll be tracking money from specific donor to ensure every shilling is spent according to their specific rules.

Compliance & Audit Prep: Ensuring the Uganda program stays ""audit-ready"" by maintaining strict documentation for those specific grants.

Stakeholder Liaison: You’ll be the translator between the ""numbers people"" and the ""program people,"" helping project managers understand their budgets."

metadata.name: Expected Manager (you can leave this blank)
Referral Cheat Sheet
metadata.value:
Here is what to consider when thinking about who to refer:
3+ years of experience in Monitoring, Evaluation, and Learning (MEL), data analysis, or program evaluation.
Experience managing medium to high complexity projects with increasing independence.
Experience managing small to medium teams and overseeing junior staff, with skills in performance evaluations and capacity building.
Proficiency in statistical analysis using Stata or R and Excel (can perform complex functions) for data cleaning, regressions, and summary statistics.
Competence in designing study plans, questionnaires, and implementing surveys, including calculating sample sizes and data analysis.
The ability to produce high-quality reports and manage project dashboards.
Experience in budget tracking, expenditure reporting, and asset management.
Represent the department in meetings and contribute to discussions on relevant platforms."

metadata.name: Expected Manager (you can leave this blank)
Referral Cheat Sheet
metadata.value:
Here is what to consider when thinking about who to refer:
The Financial Advisory Services (FAS) Specialist in Zambia supports the financial health and sustainability of One Acre Fund’s Carbon and Core programs. This role collaborates closely with local and Global Finance teams to lead budgeting, financial analysis, and reporting that directly influence program performance and organizational strategy. You will work within a two-person Zambia FAS team, consisting of a FAS Associate and this Specialist role.

metadata.name: Expected Manager (you can leave this blank)

metadata.value:

"3+ years of proven experience in sales, marketing, or adoption-focused roles within the development or social impact sector
Demonstrated experience driving user/customer adoption or behavior change. (Look for: adoption campaigns, uptake strategies, retention work, conversion funnels, farmer/beneficiary adoption.)
Experience designing or executing multi-channel marketing campaigns. Could include field activations, SMS, radio, community engagement e.t.c"

metadata.name: Expected Manager (you can leave this blank)
Nationality Criteria
metadata.value: Citizens of Countries of Operation Preferred

metadata.name: Referral Cheat Sheet
metadata.value:
Here is what to consider when thinking about who to refer:
Seeking a project-focused agronomist to manage field trials and research in Zambia. Coordinate complex agricultural experiments, troubleshoot field challenges, and generate evidence-based recommendations to increase farmer yields and profitability.The hiring manager is prioritizing operational grit over theoretical knowledge. They explicitly state that Project Management is the top priority, while deep agronomy is a "plus" that can be trained if the candidate is sharp.
